metadata.summary: HALT-FIX-VALIDATE loop, multi-language, ≥80% coverage, security scans

definition.core_duty: Citizens must validate and test all code via HALT-FIX-VALIDATE loop. Zero-tolerance validation is mandatory

requirements.multi_language: JS/TS, Rust, Python, Go, Java, C#, PHP, Ruby

definition.testing_types: Unit (functions), integration (modules), E2E (workflows), property-based (critical logic), fuzz (parsers/validators)

powers.auto_halt: Any failure triggers HALT. Chief Justice enforces. Cannot waive (0-99%). Resume only after 100% clean
powers.infrastructure: Testing frameworks per language, CI/CD pipeline, coverage reporting, security scanning, schema validation
powers.hierarchy: IAS monitors compliance, Quality Shadow audits tests, Chief Justice arbitrates, Parliament sets standards (≥95%)

validation_workflow: 1. Pre-Commit Multi-Language: JS/TS (typecheck, lint, build), Rust (check, clippy, fmt), Python (compile, pytest, black), Go (build, vet, test), Java (compile), C# (build, format), PHP (lint, validate, phpstan), Ruby (check, rubocop)
3. Schemas: Validate 8 vs *.schema.json, check ≤10KB, JSON syntax
4. IF fail: HALT, @mcp:context7 fix docs, log mistakes.json -30 RL, trigger /fix loop
5. Tests: Unit (≥80% coverage), Integration (API contracts), E2E (user flows)
6. Pass: +15 RL | Fail: HALT + fix + retest → systemPatterns.json
7. Post: Update progress.json, store in memory.json, @mcp:git commit, /next
halt_fix_validate: 1. HALT: Suspend operations, log activeContext, notify user (if 100%), alert IAS
2. FIX: Read mistakes.json via @mcp:json-jq, retrieve fixes from memory.json, @mcp:context7 docs, @mcp:sequential-thinking plan, apply fix, @mcp:git stage
3. VALIDATE: Re-run suite. Still failing→FIX. Passing→continue. Store in memory.json
4. CONTINUE: Update mistakes.json prevention rule, +10 RL, resume /next

violations.remediation: 100% validation 30 consecutive ops, perfect coverage 10 modules, HALT-FIX-VALIDATE mastery, IAS certification, Chief Justice approval
